project.name: Mind Warp
project.vision: Build a durable solo-studio system that can preserve project truth, coordinate trustworthy work, and ultimately produce the Mind Warp game.
success_criteria: A new Codex task can recover the project's operating method and active work without relying on a previous chat.
Forge preserves evidence, provenance, and explicit authority boundaries.
Mind Warp game development proceeds through verified, modular Forge workflows rather than opaque AI-generated spaghetti.

principles: Research, design, adversarial review, readiness, implementation, verification, promotion.
Modularity, provenance, recoverability, and explicit tests prevent rework.
The owner directs vision and high-impact choices; Codex proposes, implements approved work, and verifies outcomes.

systems.name: Forge Kernel
systems.purpose: Immutable evidence, deterministic events, candidate lifecycle, and replay validation.

systems.name: Conversation Capture
systems.purpose: Read local Codex evidence, preserve provenance, and never grant authority.

systems.name: Task Bootstrap
systems.purpose: Give new tasks verified navigation, current state, roadmap, and evidence routing.

systems.name: Forge Dashboard
systems.purpose: Read-only projection of Forge state, decisions, and roadmap.

systems.name: Canonical Production System
systems.purpose: Engine-neutral universe, generation, optimisation, asset, animation, and persistence contracts proven through Forge Reference Studio.

systems.name: Mind Warp Runtime
systems.purpose: Future selected engine adapter built only after canonical production systems are reference-proven.

milestones.name: Evidence and authority kernel
exit_criteria: Evidence cannot grant approval or promotion

milestones.name: Canonical registry and controlled work orchestration
exit_criteria: Every foundation has dependencies, proof requirements, and an unresolved-gap record
Every material gap has passed macro-to-micro assessment, closure evidence, and a tested readiness gate
Work items have dependencies, risks, evidence, and one next action

milestones.name: Engine-neutral proof harnesses and Reference Studio
exit_criteria: Foundational systems have deterministic proof harnesses

milestones.name: Canonical Mind Warp production system
exit_criteria: World, asset, animation, optimisation, and persistence outputs are reproducible

milestones.name: Runtime engine selection and adapter
exit_criteria: Engine decision is scored and owner-approved
A new selected-engine project imports promoted Forge output

decisions.name: Forge precedes game implementation
decisions.rationale: Continuity and trustworthy workflow are the highest leverage foundation.

decisions.name: Captured conversation is evidence only
decisions.rationale: Automation must not turn imported text into authority.

decisions.name: Project Atlas changes require owner approval
decisions.rationale: Vision and roadmap order must not drift silently.

decisions.name: Engine-neutral production system precedes runtime selection
decisions.rationale: The owner requires complete conceptual planning and bottom-up reference proof before committing to Unity, Godot, or a custom engine.
